advice: Use a password manager to generate and store unique passwords for each account.
Enable two-factor authentication on all important accounts.
Protect user data from unauthorized access.
Configure account lockout after five failed login attempts.
Set passwords to expire every 90 days and require users to create a new one.
Implement security controls using existing authentication libraries.
Encrypt sensitive data before storing it in the database.
Restrict access to sensitive resources based on user roles.
Users must follow a policy requiring strong passwords.
Apply the principle of least privilege to limit access rights.
